user_question: How do I use compile cache in torch.compile so I don't have to recompile every time I restart my script?
torch.compile stops working when I enable gradient checkpointing. Are they compatible?
How can I tell which parts of my model are being recompiled after caching with torch.compile?
Dynamo doesn't support tracing through torch.autograd.grad. I use it for computing per-sample gradients. How do I make this work with torch.compile?
I'm trying to use torch.compile with a GRU model but it doesn't seem to be supported. Is there a workaround for compiling RNN-based models?
torch.compile doesn't work inside a multiprocessing pool. I get 'daemonic processes are not allowed to have children'. How do I use compile with multiprocessing?
torch.compile throws NotImplementedError 'make_reindexer NYI on DtypeView' when I use MXFP8 quantization. Is MXFP8 supported with compile?
Does torch.compile/inductor support complex-valued tensors? I'm getting errors with complex dtypes.
How do I set up the C++ compiler for torch.compile on Windows? The default builder doesn't seem to work.
Inductor's pattern matcher silently replaces reshape with view, which changes semantics when my tensor isn't contiguous. Is this a bug?
torch.compile fails with '/usr/bin/ld: cannot find -lcuda' even though CUDA is installed. How do I fix the linker error?
How do I change the backend compiler used by torch.compile? The docs mention 'inductor' but I want to use a different one.
torch.compile raises JSONDecodeError when using compile cache with Ray and multiple GPUs. The cache seems corrupted. How do I fix this?
I'm trying to use torch.compile with DDP and AMP together but I'm getting a NotImplementedError about autocast. Is there a specific order I need to wrap my model?
torch.compile fails on both CPU and CUDA with my ResNet50 model. I'm using the nightly Docker image. Is there something wrong with my setup?
I tried torch.compile() on resnet18 but the model actually runs slower. Am I using it wrong? What should I expect on first call?
How do I use torch.compile together with Tensor Parallel? It crashes when I try to compile a tensor-parallelized model.
torch.compile doesn't seem to respect torch.cuda.set_device(). My compiled function puts tensors on the wrong GPU. Is this a known issue?
I'm getting BackendCompilerFailed with 'device kernel image is invalid' when using torch.compile with inductor. How do I fix this Triton/CUDA error?
How can I save and reuse torch.compile results across different Python processes? I don't want to recompile every time I start a new process.
I wrapped my model with torch.compile but inference is actually slower than eager mode. How do I diagnose why?
I'm seeing that some ops aren't supported by inductor. Where can I find a list of which ops are covered?
My fully compiled Qwen3 model with torchtune is 4x slower than eager mode. Why would a full model compile be slower than no compile at all?
torch.compile produces extremely slow code for complex-valued tensors. Is complex number support optimized in inductor?
The inductor-generated backward kernel for float8 scaled_grouped_mm is much slower than eager. How do I get inductor to generate efficient float8 code?
Can inductor generate native tl.dot matmuls instead of going through aten? I think the extra indirection is hurting performance.
torch.compile's decomposition of SDPA is actually faster than the EFFICIENT_ATTENTION backend on tf32. Shouldn't the fused kernel be faster?
Inductor generates suboptimal kernels when a pointwise op feeds into a reduction. The fused kernel is slower than separate kernels. How do I fix this?
torch._foreach_copy_ on CUDA tensors is much slower than a simple for-loop of tensor.copy_(). Why isn't the foreach variant faster?
Is there a way to get torch.compile to report which ops are causing slowdowns? I need some kind of performance canary or diagnostic mode.
Where can I find benchmarks for TorchInductor CPU performance? I want to compare my results against known baselines.
torch.compile makes my backward pass 100x slower for torch.cumprod. How do I figure out what's going wrong?
How do I use torch.compile with an Intel GPU? Is XPU backend supported upstream?
My simple MLP is slower with torch.compile than without. Is torch.compile not suitable for small models?

torch.compile doesn't give me any speedup and after training I get 'free(): invalid pointer' crash. What's going wrong?

The GIL isn't released when calling torch.compile kernels, which blocks my multithreaded inference pipeline. Is this expected behavior?
FlexAttention backward pass is 12x slower than the forward pass at 32K sequence length. Is there a way to profile and fix the backward scaling?

torch.compile produces different numerical output compared to eager mode. How do I debug this?
Segformer accuracy regressed when using AOT eager mode in PyTorch 2.5. How do I check if this is a known regression?
I'm seeing a 4.7% relative error in Conv2d output on CPU when using torch.compile vs eager mode. Is this level of numerical divergence expected?
torch.compile introduces significant precision errors in my model's output compared to eager. How do I figure out which operation is causing the precision loss?
torch.compile breaks reproducibility even with manual seeds set. The same code gives different results across runs when compiled.
torch.compile fails on macOS with 'omp.h file not found'. How do I fix OpenMP issues on Mac?
Inductor's epilogue fusion produces wrong results when mutations are involved. How do I debug silent correctness issues in fused kernels?
My model uses data-dependent control flow (e.g., early stopping based on values). torch.compile traces through it incorrectly. What are my options?

test_conv_stride_constraints fails on ROCm with an AssertionError. Is this a known correctness issue with conv on AMD GPUs?

Compiled flex_attention gives significantly different accuracy compared to eager. Is this expected and how do I fix it?
My RMS norm layer produces NaNs when I use torch.compile with float8 and rowwise scales. The same code works fine in eager mode. How do I debug this?
torch.compile gives different results every time I run it, even with the same input and seed. The eager version is deterministic. How do I fix this reproducibility issue?
When I compose torch.compile with torch.func.grad, I silently get a wrong result. No error is raised. How do I detect and debug silent correctness issues?
dstack followed by reciprocal gives wrong results under torch.compile but correct results in eager. Is this a known inductor bug?

torch.compile treats my optimizer as a training graph instead of inference, causing unnecessary graph breaks. How do I fix this?
I'm using DDP with Dynamo but AllReduce ops cause graph breaks. Is there a way to compile through DDP communication?
Dynamo doesn't generate guards for frozen dataclasses, so config changes don't trigger recompilation. Is this expected?

Is there a way to get Dynamo to capture a single-step graph instead of tracing through the entire forward pass?
AOTAutograd cache never hits even on a trivially simple program. Why does it always miss?

torch.compile with aotautograd doesn't support double backwards (grad of grad). Is there a workaround?
I need better guardrails for combining distributed optimizations with non-fullgraph compile. The current behavior is confusing.
torch.compile with DDP causes a graph break on _broadcast_coalesced. How do I use torch.compile with distributed training?
Dynamo doesn't support Parameter subclasses. My custom Parameter type causes a graph break.
I'm getting 'Unsupported: __self__ mismatch for bound method' graph break after upgrading PyTorch. What changed and how do I fix it?
After upgrading to PyTorch 2.4, my torch.compile code that worked before now throws errors. How do I migrate my code and fix the new graph breaks?
I used torch.compiler.disable on a function but dynamo still traces into nested functions called from it. How do I properly disable compilation for a section of my code?

Dynamo recompiles every time because it's guarding on a global config variable that I change between calls. How do I prevent guards on global state?
